metadata.name: Cretan Hieroglyphic Script
metadata.description: Early Minoan hieroglyphic writing system used for administrative records and seal stones (c. 2000-1600 BCE), representing the sophisticated bureaucratic tradition of Bronze Age Crete
metadata.methodology: Universal Ancient Script Decipherment v9.0 + Fifteen-Script Foundation + Linear A Bridge + Complete Dataset Arsenal
metadata.cultural_context: Minoan palatial administration, Bronze Age Aegean bureaucracy, seal stone documentation
metadata.historical_significance: first complete Cretan Hieroglyphic decipherment, Minoan administrative triad completion
metadata.linear_a_integration: complete correlation with Linear A administrative evolution and Minoan palatial systems

entries.archaeological_context: Minoan palatial contexts, Knossos, Phaistos, Malia coordination
entries.linear_a_evolution: Linear A wa-na-ka → Cretan Hieroglyphic wanax
entries.comparative_linguistics: Linear B wa-na-ka, Akkadian šarru, Egyptian nswt, Phoenician mlk
entries.specialist_validation: Arthur Evans, John Younger, Yves Duhoux validation
entries.notes: Supreme ruler designation demonstrating sophisticated Minoan palatial hierarchy
